name: Pheasant with Black Olives
recipeInstructions:
1. To prepare the pheasant, remove breast meat and thighs by cutting along each side of the breastbone with a boning knife. Scrape down the rib cage to detach the meat from the bone. Leave wings attached.
2. Loosen the skin between the knee and top of the breast, then slip it around to the small of the back. Cut through the knee joint where the leg-thigh meets the spine. Separate the boneless thigh portion (skin-on) and the skin-on boneless breast with wing from the pheasant. Reserve bones for making stock.
3. Combine marinade ingredients in a bowl and pour over the pheasant pieces, marinating for 2 to 3 hours.
4. To make the sauce:
a. Crush reserved bones using the blunt edge of a heavy knife.
b. In a 5-quart stock pot, heat olive oil until it smokes lightly.
c. Add crushed bones and brown them by stirring constantly for about 10 minutes, until they turn golden brown.
d. Add onions and garlic and cook for an additional 10 minutes until caramelized and the bones are browned.
e. Remove excess oil from the pot.
f. Pour in Zinfandel and stir to loosen browned bits.
g. Reduce heat to medium and simmer, reducing the liquid's volume and thickening it into a syrupy consistency.
h. Add chicken stock and bring to a boil, then reduce until reduced to about 3 or 4 cups. Strain to remove solids.
5. When ready to serve, reheat the sauce with olives and tomatoes. Season as desired with salt and pepper.
6. Remove pheasant from marinade, scraping off any remaining solids on the meat. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Heat a pan over medium-high heat and sear the skin side of the pheasant until golden brown. Flip and repeat for even browning.
8. Roast the pheasant in an oven preheated to 400°F (200°C) for 10 to 12 minutes, or until fully cooked. The breast should feel firm and tender when touched, while thigh meat requires additional cooking time.
9. Plate one pheasant breast and one thigh per person and drizzle with the olive sauce.
